title: eschscholzia lemmonii subsp. kernensis
intro: Eschscholzia lemmonii subsp. kernensis, also known as Kern California poppy or Kern golden poppy, is a plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is a subspecies of the California poppy (Eschscholzia californica). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and muscle cramps. The health benefits of Eschscholzia lemmonii subsp. kernensis include its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation, as well as its potential to ease symptoms of menopause and other hormonal imbalances. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the flowers and making teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
